gloss:
Statement of record: `Eidometry.Thesis.width_support_invariant` (Eidometry thesis, section Orthogonality).

The residual spread of any metric-valued quantity across the states you cannot tell apart is the same for any two measurement families that collapse the state space the same way. How much you remain uncertain about a function of the state is fixed by the collapsed structure alone, not by the choice of measurements.

Discharged by [MTH.R-2026-1183].

statement.pretty: ∀ {S : Type u_1} {ι : Type u_2} {V : ι → Type u_3} {Y : Type u_4} [inst : PseudoEMetricSpace Y] {T : (i : ι) → S → V i} {ι' : Type u_5} {V' : ι' → Type u_6} {T' : (i : ι') → S → V' i}, WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim {id} T = WMSpec.Transform.wordBisim {id} T' → ∀ (F : S → Y) (s₀ : S), WMSpec.Transform.fiberWidth F (WMSpec.Transform.fiberOf T s₀) = WMSpec.Transform.fiberWidth F (WMSpec.Transform.fiberOf T' s₀)

title: Eidometry.Thesis.width_support_invariant
